title: Filix-mas effusa
intro:
I couldn't find any information about Filix-mas effusa. However, Filix-mas is another name for the plant Dryopteris filix-mas, also known as the male fern. 

Here's a paragraph about Dryopteris filix-mas:

Dryopteris filix-mas, also known as the male fern, is a perennial fern species commonly found in woodlands and hedgerows across Europe. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of the male fern include flavonoids, saponins, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with the male fern include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, often used to treat various health conditions.
